mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy falls below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and falling housing starts indicates weakening consumer confidence and shifting demand away from durable goods, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects strong capacity utilization and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in less than 45 percent of late-cycle acceleration episodes, or the signal loses predictive power during high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth crossing above 3.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC turns positive during high-inflation periods, or 20-day forward returns exceed zero in more than 60 percent of threshold-crossing events.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly correlates with expanding corporate revenue bases and wage-driven consumption, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative alpha over any 12-month forward window, or payroll growth shows zero correlation with subsequent equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and acceleration in consumer prices creates a stagflationary environment that suppresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns with greater than 55 percent accuracy, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production coinciding with falling federal funds rates amplifies profit margin expansion and inventory restocking, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 5-day horizons, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant across multiple market regimes.
